events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON. Need infer schedule impact. Scenario image is lookahead schedule and hold notice. Need list activity IDs crews can start now: not blocked by held activity directly or transitively, with all other predecessors complete. Hold date day 23. Activity F Electrical rough ON HOLD indefinitely as of day 23. Activities finishing by day 23 are complete; nothing else has started. Need determine which activities can start now.

Let's parse schedule. Activities A to Q. Predecessors listed. Durations. Early start bars. Blue=work, hatched=cure lag, red=inspection hold.

Given assumption: all activities whose bars finish by day 23 are complete; nothing else started. Hold: F (Electrical rough) is on hold indefinitely as of day 23. Need find activities not blocked by held activity directly or transitively, with all other predecessors complete. So any activity whose predecessors all complete (by day 23) and not dependent on F (or on any descendant of F). But need consider cure lags? They are activities like CC (Slab cure) which is a lag cannot compress. Predecessor relationship CC -> D. Bars finishing by day 23 complete includes cure lags? The assumption says all activities whose bars finish by day 23 are complete. Need examine positions.

Let's read chart approximate. Workdays 0-45. Activities:
A Mobilize & layout [2d] no pred. Bar starts day 0 length2 => finishes day2.
B Underground rough-in [5d] pred A. Starts ~day2, ends day7.
C Slab pour [3d] pred B. Starts day7 ends day10.
CC Slab cure [3d] pred C. Hatched bar days ~10-13. Cure lag.
D Framing [7d] pred CC. Starts ~day13 ends day20.
E Roof dry-in [3d] pred D. Starts day20 ends day23.
F Electrical rough [5d] pred E. Starts day23 ends day28. Red at day23? Actually I is red, F blue starts day23.
G Plumbing top-out [4d] pred E. Starts day23 ends day27.
H HVAC rough [3d] pred E. Starts day23 ends day26.
I Rough inspection [1d] pred F,G,H. Red square at day28? Actually after F/G/H.
J Insulation [2d] pred I. Starts day29 ends31.
K Insulation inspection [1d] pred J. Red at day31.
L Drywall hang [2d] pred K. starts ~32 ends34.
M Tape & finish [5d] pred L. starts34 ends39.
N Paint [2d] pred M. starts39 ends41.
O Trim carpentry [2d] pred N. starts41 ends43.
P Flooring [3d] pred N. starts41? Actually P starts after N? Schedule shows P [3d] pred N, bar overlapping? It starts ~41 ends44.
Q Final inspection [1d] pred O,P. red at45.

Hold notice: Activity F (Electrical rough) is ON HOLD indefinitely as of day 23 (materials unavailable). Activities finishing by day 23 are complete. Wait "as of day 23" maybe at start of day23. The bar for F starts day23. Since F is held, it won't proceed. G and H also start day23 pred E completed. They are not blocked by F directly? They depend on E only. But are they already started? The assumption says "Assume all activities whose bars finish by the hold date are complete; nothing else has started." The hold date day23. Activities finishing by day23: A,B,C,CC,D,E maybe complete. Activities starting at day23 (F,G,H) have not started (nothing else has started). The question: "List, in the workable field, the IDs of every activity the crews can start now: not blocked by the held activity directly or transitively, with all other predecessors complete." So now is after hold at day23. We can start any activity whose predecessors are all complete and not blocked by F transitively. G and H have predecessor E complete, and are not blocked by F. So G and H can start. I depends on F,G,H so blocked. J depends on I -> blocked. All later blocked transitively. What about F itself? It's on hold, cannot start. So workable: G, H. Also maybe activity E is already complete. No other activities have predecessors all complete besides G,H? D, E etc already complete. A through E complete. CC complete. So only ready-to-start activities are F,G,H. F blocked; G,H not. So answer workable ["G","H"].

Need also determine decision/risk/work_stage etc. Since schedule has a hold causi…
